summary: Matched the first slice of Popo/Ice Climbers side-special code in `ftPp_SpecialS.c`, raising the file report to 8.44% matched / 14.42 KB. The PR added C for several 100% functions including `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F68C`, `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F6FC`, `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F964`, `ftPp_SpecialS_Enter`, `ftPp_SpecialAirS_Enter`, `ftPp_SpecialS1_Anim`, and `ftPp_SpecialS2_Anim`. It also converted related Popo/Nana headers from generic `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*`, added Popo side-special motion vars and Ice Climber attribute fields, and marked corresponding scratch entries as OK.
what_changed: Added matched C implementations for Popo side-special entry and animation helper functions in `ftPp_SpecialS.c`.

Implemented side-special setup paths that initialize command variables, Popo motion vars, velocity, `x1A5C` partner references, hitlag callbacks, and motion states for grounded and aerial side-special variants.

Implemented animation cleanup paths that clear damage/death/hitlag callbacks, clear `x1A5C`, call `ft_8008A2BC`, and in the partner-linked S2 animation path also reset the referenced Nana/partner state when needed.
Added `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F964` as a motion-id check returning false for `ftPp_MS_SpecialS2` and `ftPp_MS_SpecialAirS2`.
Changed many related Popo and Nana function prototypes from `HSD_GObj*` to the more specific `Fighter_GObj*` and included `ft/forward.h` where needed.
Added `ftNn_Init_80123954(Fighter_GObj* gobj, GroundOrAir pp_ga)` prototype to `ftNn_Init.h` for Popo side-special partner-state selection.

Expanded `ftIceClimberAttributes` with typed fields at offsets including `x20`, `x24`, `x28`, `x2C`, and `x68`, retained size with padding, and added `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(ftIceClimberAttributes) == 0x15C)`.
Added `union ftPp_MotionVars` with `specials` fields mapped at `fp+2340` through `fp+235C`, then wired it into the main `Fighter` motion-vars union as `pp`.
Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` entries for addresses `0x8011F68C`, `0x8011F6FC`, `0x8011F964`, `0x8011F99C`, `0x8011FB08`, `0x8011FC78`, and `0x8011FCD0` from percentage statuses to `OK`.
The assembly listing for `ftPp_SpecialS.s` was regenerated/rebased with real addresses and branch targets such as `.L_8011F6DC` instead of local zero-based labels.
smart_moves: Used small static inline helpers (`inlineA0`, `inlineA1`, `inlineA2`, `inlineB0`, `resetAnim`, `inlineC0`, `inlineC1`) to express repeated setup/cleanup sequences while preserving the compiler shape needed for matching.
Modeled Popo-specific motion variables directly under `fp->mv.pp.specials`, which avoids raw offset writes for the side-special state block at `fp+2340`.
Typed the Ice Climber special attributes as floats/ints instead of relying on opaque filler, enabling velocity and timer fields to be referenced semantically while preserving the known struct size.
Replaced a local `extern float const ftPp_Init_804D9848` usage with available includes and used literal `0` in the `ftParts_8007592C` calls where that matched the generated code.
Kept partner/Nana interactions explicit through `Player_GetEntityAtIndex(fp->player_id, 1)` and `ftNn_Init_80123954`, making the Ice Climbers dual-character behavior visible in C.
Used `PAD_STACK` in entry and animation functions where needed to match stack layout.
decomp_lessons: For fighter code, prefer `Fighter_GObj*` over generic `HSD_GObj*` in prototypes once call sites are known to operate on fighters; this reduces casts and supports `GET_FIGHTER`-based matching.
When matching fighter move code, adding the character-specific motion-vars union to `Fighter` can be necessary before clean C can replace raw `fp+2340` accesses.

Side-special entry functions often require both gameplay setup and callback setup; in this PR the entry functions run motion-state selection, callback initialization, `ftAnim_8006EBA4`, side-special vars, then callback initialization again.
Ice Climbers code frequently needs to handle the partner GObj through `x1A5C` and `Player_GetEntityAtIndex`; cleanup must clear both Popo's and, when present, the partner's references/flags.
Preserve struct size with explicit padding and `STATIC_ASSERT` when only a subset of fields is understood. This PR names useful fields in `ftIceClimberAttributes` while keeping the 0x15C size stable.
Scratch entries should be downgraded from numeric match percentages to `OK` when corresponding functions become accepted/matched in source.
naming_conventions: New typed Popo side-special state was named `ftPp_MotionVars` with nested `ftPp_SpecialSVars specials` and offset-style temporary fields `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x14`, `x18`, `x1C`.
Ice Climber attribute struct was normalized from `_ftIceClimberAttributes` to `ftIceClimberAttributes`.
Matched but still semantically unknown functions retained address-based names such as `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F68C`, `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F6FC`, and `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F964`.
Action-state functions use the existing pattern `ftPp_SpecialS_Enter`, `ftPp_SpecialAirS_Enter`, `ftPp_SpecialS1_Anim`, and `ftPp_SpecialS2_Anim`.
Temporary match-shaping helpers were kept file-local and generic (`inlineA0`, `inlineA1`, etc.), signaling they are not API names and may be refactored later.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(4 * 6)` and `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)` in functions that needed stack padding to match.
Factored repeated callback assignments into inline helpers so the compiler could duplicate the sequence at call sites.
Used literal zero arguments for `ftParts_8007592C(fp, 0, 0)` instead of referencing the Popo zero float symbol where that produced the matched code shape.
Introduced exact offset-backed struct fields and motion vars to turn raw loads/stores like `fp+2340`/`fp+235C` into C member accesses without changing layout.
The regenerated asm diff shows real address labels and resolved branch/call displacements, useful for verifying matched functions against addresses `8011F68C` through `8011FCD0`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context, so there is no human review feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace temporary `inlineA0`/`inlineB0` helper names in `ftPp_SpecialS.c` with semantic helper names.
Search for additional work on `ftPp_SpecialS_8011F720`, `ftPp_SpecialAirS1_Anim`, and the remaining unmatched Popo side-special physics/collision functions.
Check whether `ftIceClimberAttributes` fields `x20`, `x24`, `x28`, `x2C`, and `x68` receive semantic names in later decomp PRs.
Look for follow-up PRs that further type Popo/Nana headers from `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*` or add additional Ice Climbers motion vars.
